channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Building a Production-Ready Serverless App on Google Cloud (Part 1: Architecture)
item.description: The Problem In my previous post, I shared how I used an AI agent framework during a train ride to build a Proof of Concept (POC) for a project called the Dog Finder App. The response was great,...

item.title: Enterprise Blockchain in TypeScript: Real-World Case Studies, Protocol Mappings, MPC, HSM & Post-Quantum Patterns That Actually Run
item.description: Most enterprise blockchain projects die in PoC hell. You've seen it: a flashy demo, some Hyperledger slides, a proof-of-concept that "works on my machine," and then... silence. The pilot never...

item.title: Mastering Quantum Logic with Quantum Studio
item.description: What is Quantum Computing? At its core, Quantum Computing is a fundamentally different way of processing information. While classical computers (the one you're using right now) use bits—which are...

item.title: How to Generate Website Screenshots with Python in 3 Lines of Code
item.description: Ever needed to capture a screenshot of a website programmatically? Maybe for generating link previews, monitoring pages, or creating PDF reports. Here's how to do it with Python in just a few...

item.title: 51万行源码泄露：全面解构 Claude Code 如何成为 AI 编程天花板
item.description: 51万行源码泄露：全面解构 Claude Code 如何成为 AI 编程天花板 原文链接：https://warpnav.com/claude-code-source-map 2026年3月31日，安全研究者 Chaofan Shou 发现了一个“史诗级”的构建疏漏。 Anthropic 发布到 npm 的 Claude Code (v2.1.88) 包体积从 17MB 暴涨到了...

item.title: Database Sharding in System Design
item.description: What Is Database Sharding Database Sharding represents a fundamental horizontal scaling technique in system design that divides a single large database into multiple smaller, independent shards....

item.title: Three Things Had to Align: The Real Story Behind the LLM Revolution
item.description: ChatGPT didn't come out of nowhere. It's the result of 60 years of dead ends, one accidental breakthrough, and three completely separate technologies all maturing at exactly the same moment....

item.title: REST vs GraphQL vs WebSockets vs Webhooks: A Real-World Decision Guide (With Code)
item.description: You have used all of these. But when someone asks you, maybe in an interview, or in a system design meeting, why you chose WebSockets over polling, or webhooks over a queue, can you answer...

item.title: Your Ruby CSV Import Ran Successfully — Your Data May Still Be Wrong
item.description: Are you sure that Ruby CSV imported all your data — and correctly? 🤔 I wasn't looking for bugs. I was improving the performance of smarter_csv, then added a new round of tests — including some...

item.title: Yupp shuts down after raising $33M from a16z crypto’s Chris Dixon
item.description: Less than a year after launching, with checks from some of the biggest names in Silicon Valley, crowdsourced AI model feedback startup Yupp is closing its business, the company said Tuesday.

item.title: Samsung’s new app claims to alleviate motion sickness using sound
item.description: Samsung released a new free app today called Hearapy, now available for Android devices through the Google Play store, that it claims can reduce the symptoms of motion sickness using just sound....

item.title: Anker’s power bank with built-in cables is one of my favorite gadgets, and it’s cheaper than usual
item.description: Anker’s Laptop Power Bank is one of the more useful gadgets I’ve bought in recent years. I know, it’s just a portable battery, but it’s packed with power and thoughtful features that are tough to...

item.title: The Super Mario Galaxy Movie is beautiful but a little too busy
item.description: With The Super Mario Bros. Movie, Nintendo entered the next phase of its plan to become a different kind of entertainment giant. Though it wasn't the company's first go at putting its iconic...

item.title: Evercade’s new handheld has a larger screen and dual thumbsticks for 3D games
item.description: After releasing smaller versions of its handhelds, Evercade has announced a new portable console called the Nexus featuring a large 5.89-inch screen. That's larger than the 4.3-inch screen on...

item.title: No Kings is taking back Americana
item.description: "I was hoping we were going to have a bigger crowd than this, especially with the nice weather and everything," Michael Maria told me. "I'm a little disappointed because the last march, I think...

item.title: Android Developer Verification Rollout Begins Ahead of September Enforcement
item.description: Google on Monday said it's officially rolling out Android developer verification to all developers to combat the problem of bad actors distributing harmful apps while "hiding behind anonymity."...

item.title: Quantum computers need vastly fewer resources than thought to break vital encryption
item.description: No, the sky isn't falling, but Q Day is coming, and it won't be as expensive as thought.

item.title: The Galaxy S26’s photo app can sloppify your memories
item.description: The Google Pixel 9 walked so that the Samsung Galaxy S26 could run. Google introduced AI editing tools to Photos slowly. It started with changes to the background - make the sky more blue, or...

item.title: Hisense’s 75-inch U6 mini-LED TV is cheaper than its 65-inch model
item.description: If you’re keen to get a new 4K TV that has built-in streaming services provided by Amazon’s Fire TV software, it’s tough to get more for your money than with Hisense’s U6. The company’s 75-inch,...

item.title: Alexa+ gets new food ordering experiences with Uber Eats and Grubhub
item.description: You can now order from Uber Eats and Grubhub using Alexa+, an experience Amazon says will be similar to chatting with a waiter at a restaurant or placing an order at a drive-thru.

item.title: The Sonos Play and mic-less Era 100 SL are now available
item.description: After a couple of quiet years, Sonos is back with two new consumer speakers: the portable Sonos Play and the more affordable Era 100 SL. They retail for $299 and $189, respectively, and both are...

item.title: The latest Matter update improves camera streaming
item.description: The Connectivity Standards Alliance (CSA) released Matter 1.5.1 today, building upon the camera and video doorbell support added as part of the Matter 1.5 update last November. Although 1.5.1 is a...

item.title: TrueConf Zero-Day Exploited in Attacks on Southeast Asian Government Networks
item.description: A high-severity security flaw in the TrueConf client video conferencing software has been exploited in the wild as a zero-day as part of a campaign targeting government entities in Southeast Asia...

item.title: Build with Veo 3.1 Lite, our most cost-effective video generation model
item.description: Build with Veo 3.1 Lite

item.title: Granite 4.0 3B Vision: Compact Multimodal Intelligence for Enterprise Documents
Nomadic raises $8.4 million to wrangle the data pouring off autonomous vehicles
item.description: The company turns footage from robots into structured, searchable datasets with a deep learning model.

item.title: Shifting to AI model customization is an architectural imperative
item.description: In the early days of large language models (LLMs), we grew accustomed to massive 10x jumps in reasoning and coding capability with every new model iteration. Today, those jumps have flattened into...

item.title: Exclusive: Runway launches $10M fund, Builders program to support early-stage AI startups
item.description: Runway is launching a $10 million fund and startup program to back companies building with its AI video models, as it pushes toward interactive, real-time “video intelligence” applications.

item.title: Vertex AI Vulnerability Exposes Google Cloud Data and Private Artifacts
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed a security "blind spot" in Google Cloud's Vertex AI platform that could allow artificial intelligence (AI) agents to be weaponized by an attacker to gain...
